title: On minimal non-(torsion-by-nilpotent) and non-((locally finite)-by-nilpotent) groups
abstract:
Let
                    Ω
                    be a class of groups. A group is said to be minimal non-
                    Ω
                    if it is not an
                    Ω
                    -group, while all its proper subgroups belong to
                    Ω
                    . In this Note we prove that a minimal non-(torsion-by-nilpotent) (respectively, non-((locally finite)-by-nilpotent)) group
                    G
                    is a finitely generated perfect group which has no proper subgroup of finite index and such that
                    
                      G
                      /
                      Frat
                      (
                      G
                      )
                    
                    is an infinite simple group, where
                    
                      Frat
                      (
                      G
                      )
                    
                    stands for the Frattini subgroup of
                    G
                    .
